text: we present a case of combined endolaparoscopic surgery for colon polyp. This is a forty-two year old patient who underwent alloparoscopic assisted vaginal hysterectomy in twenty thirteen. Subsequently, in twenty seventeen, alloparoscopic appendectomy with the result of mucinous cystadenoma. A CT colonography was performed in which a one centimeter polyp was seen in the cecum with a very redundant and folded sigmoid colon in the pelvis as well as unassociated diverticulitis. After two colonoscopies, progression to the left colon was impossible due to the presence of a fixed sigmoid loop. Patient is referred for laparoscopic ileocythectomy.

The CT colonography images show a one-centimeter polyp in the cecum, visible in both axial and volume-rendered views. The video then displays adhesions of the sigmoid colon to the abdominal wall in the right iliac fossa and pelvis. The surgeon performs division of attachments on the lateral abdominal wall and pelvis, resulting in complete mobilization of the sigmoid colon.

The surgeon introduces the colonoscope into the sigmoid colon, using laparoscopic clamps to assist its advancement. The colonoscope progresses through the ascending colon and then posteriorly navigates the splenic flexure towards the transverse colon.

The endoscopic view shows the cecum with a polyp on the lateral wall, appearing as a submucosal nodule with preserved mucosa. Adrenaline with Mechelin Blue is injected submucosally to assess the depth and define the edges of the lesion. A polypectomy loop is then inserted, and the tumor is excised with laparoscopic compression of the intestinal wall.

The surgeon places approximation clips on the edges of the resected bowel wall to ensure closure, then retrieves the specimen using a basket rod net. The endoscopically resected specimen shows signs of foreign body reaction, necrosis, and calcification.
